sources: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

items.headline: China grants itself power to probe and punish foreign supply chain restrictions
items.body: Beijing published sweeping rules allowing government agencies to investigate foreign nations that impose discriminatory bans or harm Chinese supply chains, with authority to retaliate. The framework, released by the State Council, targets espionage and enables China to counter trade curbs from the United States and Europe.

items.headline: Hungary votes April 12 as Orban faces first serious challenge in 16 years
items.body: Opposition Tisza Party leads most independent polls by double digits ahead of parliamentary elections, though the electoral system heavily favors the ruling Fidesz party. Outcome will shape European Union cohesion on Ukraine support and test whether entrenched state capture can be reversed through elections alone.

items.headline: Afghanistan Pakistan conflict displaces 94,000 as China mediates talks in Urumqi
items.body: Cross-border fighting that began in February has left hundreds dead and cut off 100,000 people in two Afghan districts from essential supplies, according to UN humanitarian office. Both sides agreed not to escalate and to explore comprehensive solution after seven days of Chinese mediation.

items.headline: North Korea tested cluster warhead missiles as weapons development accelerates
items.body: Pyongyang announced testing of ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads and other new weapons systems this week. The spree pushes expansion of nuclear-capable forces as regional tensions remain elevated.
